brand.name: Old Landmark Publishing
brand.vertical: Independent ebook publishing — retail ebook store (religious, AI/business, cozy fantasy) plus end-to-end author publishing & marketing services

summary: Old Landmark Publishing runs two businesses from one site: a retail store selling affordable niche ebooks (religious/Christian, AI & business, and cozy fantasy titles, free to $17), and a done-for-you publishing service that turns self-publishing authors' manuscripts into designed, distributed, and marketed ebooks. It serves two audiences: budget-conscious readers seeking niche titles, and independent or first-time authors who want professional publishing support without a traditional publisher.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Activate every new free-ebook downloader and first-time buyer — get them reading the book they just claimed and aware that Old Landmark is both a store and a publishing partner.
campaigns.trigger: Free ebook download (AI-Proof Your Life or The Opal and the Rose excerpt) or first paid ebook purchase. Send within seconds of the download/checkout event.
campaigns.segment: New contacts; branch on which lead magnet/title they claimed (AI/business vs. cozy fantasy vs. religious) to set the genre voice and the relevant next read.
campaigns.rationale: Today every free-ebook downloader is left completely cold — the single biggest gap on the site. A welcome email is the most-opened send a brand makes and is the only thing turning anonymous free-download traffic into a known, re-marketable audience.

emails.purpose: Deliver the free book instantly, welcome warmly, set expectations, and plant the dual identity (great reads + a publishing team for authors) without hard-selling.
emails.subject: Your free book is ready — start reading in one tap
emails.preview: Plus a quick hello from the team behind Old Landmark Publishing.
keyPoints: One-tap link to the exact title they claimed; confirm it's theirs to keep, free, forever
Branch the welcome line by genre — reverent for religious readers, practical for AI/business, cozy for fantasy
Set expectations: a few short emails with reading picks and ideas, reply-to is a real person
Soft one-line note for authors: 'Writing a book of your own? We help authors publish, distribute, and market — just reply.'

campaigns.name: Author Consultation-to-Client Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert author publishing-service inquiries into a booked conversation and a signed engagement — the highest-LTV revenue play on the site.
campaigns.trigger: Contact form submitted or live-chat inquiry about publishing services. Send the hero email within minutes while intent is hot.
campaigns.segment: Authors who expressed interest in publishing/marketing services; branch by stated stage (finished manuscript vs. idea/in-progress).
campaigns.rationale: Author services are custom-quoted and far higher value than $2.50–$17 ebooks, yet nothing moves an inquiry from 'contact form submitted' to signed client. A fast, confidence-building first reply directly attacks the #1 objection — 'can a small indie publisher actually get my book seen?'

emails.purpose: Respond fast, frame the end-to-end outcome, dissolve the credibility objection with proof, and drive to a single low-friction next step — a real conversation.
emails.subject: Let's turn your manuscript into a published book
emails.preview: Here's exactly how we get your book onto Kindle, B&N — and in front of readers.
keyPoints: Acknowledge their inquiry by name and restate their goal: a professionally published, distributed, sold book
Walk the managed path in three plain steps — design & formatting, multi-platform distribution, post-launch marketing
Defuse the reach objection with the 4.8/5 from 150+ reviews and concrete distribution channels (Amazon Kindle, Barnes & Noble)
One CTA: book a no-pressure conversation where the team builds their custom publishing plan

campaigns.name: Free-Reader Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move free-ebook downloaders toward a paid purchase, and surface the publishing service to readers who are secretly authors.
campaigns.trigger: Free ebook downloaded; contact has not yet purchased. Runs after the welcome, ~every 10 days.
campaigns.segment: Free-download subscribers below a 'buyer' threshold; branch by the genre they downloaded (religious, AI/business, cozy fantasy).
campaigns.rationale: Two strong lead magnets feed the top of the funnel but nothing converts a free reader into a $2.50–$17 buyer or a publishing lead. A genre-matched nurture is the engine that monetizes the existing free-download traffic.

emails.purpose: The evaluation/proof beat — bridge from the free book to the paid catalog in the same genre, using reviews and a clear value framing to earn the first purchase.
emails.subject: If you liked your free read, you'll love these three
emails.preview: Hand-picked titles in your genre — most under the price of a coffee.
keyPoints: Open by referencing the specific free book they read and the theme it explored
Recommend 3 paid titles in the same niche, each with a one-line hook and a review snippet (4.8/5)
Anchor the affordability — niche titles from $2.50, not $25 from a big publisher
Footer note for authors: 'Loved a book and thinking of writing one? See how we publish authors.'

campaigns.name: Reader Win-Back
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed one-time buyers and dormant free subscribers with personalized genre recommendations, and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: 60–90 days of no opens, clicks, or purchases.
campaigns.segment: Dormant subscribers and past one-time ebook buyers; personalize on the genre of their last download/purchase.
campaigns.rationale: One-time buyers vanish after a single purchase with no path back, and there's no loyalty mechanism. Genre-personalized win-back is proven for niche-catalog brands (a streaming case hit 38% open / 2.53% CTR / 500% ROI) and recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts cheaply.

emails.purpose: A 'we miss you' opener that leads with a personalized new-release pick in their favorite genre to give a concrete reason to come back.
emails.subject: New cozy fantasy you haven't read yet
emails.preview: We saved a few fresh titles in your favorite genre — take a look?
emails.cta: See what's new in your genre
keyPoints: Dynamic subject/body swaps the genre to match their history (religious, AI/business, or cozy fantasy)
Lead with 2–3 new or popular titles they haven't bought, not a discount
Warm, low-pressure 'we've added new reads since you last visited' framing
Quiet sunset signal for non-responders downstream to keep sender reputation clean

campaigns.name: The Old Landmark Reading Room (Value Newsletter)
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind across a catalog with no subscription, build authority in each niche, and drive repeat purchases plus author leads without discounting.
campaigns.trigger: Scheduled cadence (every 2 weeks), editioned by reader interest.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding and route the recently-dormant to Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: With no subscription or membership, repeat revenue depends entirely on staying memorable between releases — and there's currently no newsletter at all. A recurring value email is the always-on engine that drives repeat ebook sales and steady author inquiries.

emails.purpose: The flagship recurring edition that establishes the habit: one useful idea or story per send, a single featured read, and a soft author CTA.
emails.subject: 3 quiet new releases worth your weekend
emails.preview: This issue: one idea, one featured book, and a peek behind a launch.
keyPoints: Recurring structure: one idea/tip in the reader's niche + one featured title (80% value / 20% offer)
Edition the lead block by interest tag — faith, AI & income, or fantasy
Single primary CTA (read the featured book); secondary author CTA in the footer
Build the habit with a named recurring section so readers learn to expect it
